mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) directly signals increasing consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC below 0.02 or hit rate below 55% over any trailing 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates tightening labor conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of trigger events over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low federal funds rates and low inflation compress real yields, making equities more attractive relative to fixed income and boosting stock prices over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The composite signal of low rates and low inflation does not produce positive average 20-day forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production is a positive signal for equities only during early-cycle expansion when output is below potential; in late-cycle overheating it can precede tightening and negative returns.
regime_hint: early expansion with negative output gap
failure_condition: During periods with negative output gap, rising INDPRO leads to negative 60-day forward returns more than 30% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1 million units signal robust construction activity and economic momentum, which tends to lift equity prices over a one-week horizon as market sentiment improves.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and retail sales indicate a virtuous cycle of rising employment, income, and consumption that drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect (simultaneous rise) yields negative 20-day returns in more than one-third of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation from a very low base (CPI year-over-year below 2%) signals that demand is recovering, which is interpreted positively by equity markets over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: In periods with CPI YoY below 2%, rising CPI leads to negative 20-day returns in a majority of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP signals broad economic expansion that directly supports corporate profits and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon, making it a reliable positive trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC over any trailing 5-year window is below 0.00 (i.e., no directional predictive power).
